AssignApplication.description: The application with the ID `0892c36d-40df-4f26-923b-76b5d213b420` is assigned to the device.
AssignApplication.summary: Assign an application to a device

AssignLocation.description: The location with the ID `57f2b22e-7923-497b-8120-fd2b94579f13` is assigned as the location for the device.
AssignLocation.summary: Assign a location to a device

AssignNonWorkspaceUserContact.description: A non-workspace user contact is assigned to the device with the email

AssignSubscription.description: The subscription with the ID `3292c36d-30ef-4f26-823b-76b5d213b670` is assigned to the device.
AssignSubscription.summary: Assign a subscription to a device

AssignWorkspaceUserContact.description: The workspace user with the ID `4a2e4102-5c05-4713-8fc8-4e82f9704e53` is assigned as the contact for the device.

CreateNewTags.description: The `location` tag is created with `San Jose`, the `purchase month` tag is created with `April 2023` , and the `Setup Option` tag is created with `Default`.
CreateNewTags.summary: Create new tags for a device

CreateNewTagsAndAssignApplication.description: The `location` tag is created with `San Jose`, the `purchase month` tag is created with `April 2023`, and the `Setup Option` tag is created with `Default`. The application with the ID `0892c36d-40df-4f26-923b-76b5d213b420` is assigned to the device.
CreateNewTagsAndAssignApplication.summary: Create new tags and assign an application to a device

DeleteExistingTags.summary: Remove existing tags from a device

GroupByCategoryExample.description: Devices are grouped by the `category` attribute, and the count of devices for each category is returned.

GroupByIsFlexExample.description: Devices are grouped by the `isFlex` attribute, and the count of flex and non-flex devices is returned.

GroupByMakeExample.description: Devices are grouped by the `make` attribute, and the count of devices for each make is returned.

GroupByManufacturedDateExample.description: Devices are grouped by the `manufacturedDate` attribute, with upper and lower threshold applied. Returns four buckets

GroupByOwnershipExample.description: Devices are grouped by the `ownership` attribute, and the count of devices for each ownership type is returned.

GroupBySelectMakeExample.description: Devices are grouped by the selected `make` attributes, and distinct values are returned.
GroupBySelectMakeExample.summary: Group devices by select to give distinct makes

GroupBySourceExample.description: Devices are grouped by the `source` attribute, and the count of devices for each source type is returned.

UnassignApplication.description: The application is removed from the device.
UnassignApplication.summary: Remove an application from a device

UnassignContact.description: The contact is unassigned from the device.
UnassignContact.summary: Unassign a contact from a device

UnassignLocation.description: The location is unassigned from the device.
UnassignLocation.summary: Unassign a location from a device

UnassignSubscription.description: The subscription is unassigned from the device.
UnassignSubscription.summary: Unassign a subscription from a device

UpdateAndDeleteExistingTags.description: The `location` tag is updated with `San Francisco` and the `Setup Option` tag is removed.
UpdateAndDeleteExistingTags.summary: Update existing tags with new values and remove existing tags

UpdateExistingTagsAndUnassignApplication.description: The `location` tag is updated with `Los Angeles` and the `Setup Option` tag is updated with `Simplified`. The application is removed from the device.
UpdateExistingTagsAndUnassignApplication.summary: Update existing tags with new values and remove an application from a device

UpdateExistingTagsWithNewTagValues.description: The `location` tag is updated with `Los Angeles` and the `Setup Option` tag is updated with `Simplified`.
UpdateExistingTagsWithNewTagValues.summary: Update existing tags with new values

UpdateLocation.description: The location of the device is updated to the location with the ID `57f2b22e-7923-497b-8120-fd2b94579f13`.
UpdateLocation.summary: Update the location of a device

UpdateSupportDetails.description: The support level of the device is updated to `Proactive Care` and the support end date is updated to `2024-12-31T23:59:59Z`.
UpdateSupportDetails.summary: Update the support information of a device

UpdateTags.description: The `purchase month` tag is updated with `April 2023`, and the `Setup Option` tag is updated with `Default`.

UpdateThirdPartyDevice.description: Update missing details such as tags, location and warranty information.

LocationHeader.description: The URI of the accepted asynchronous operation resource

LocationHeaderV1.description: The URI of the accepted asynchronous operation resource

endedAt.description: Date and time the asynchronous operation ended in UTC format.

id.description: The unique identifier of the device.

progressPercent.description: A number that indicates how close to completion the asynchronous operation is.

result.description: An array that provides information on successful or unsuccessful operations.

resultType.description: Relates individual devices to a result type. A result type declares if an operation was successful or unsuccessful.

startedAt.description: Date and time the asynchronous operation began in UTC format.

status.description: The current status of an asynchronous operation.

suggestedPollingIntervalSeconds.description: The suggested time to wait (in minutes) before calling the operation again.

timeoutMinutes.description: The number of minutes it took for the operation to time out.

status.description: Three digit HTTPS status code and message.

transactionId.description: The unique identifier of the transaction.

issues.description: An array of request issues.

archived.description: A boolean that indicates whether the device has been archived or not. Archived devices are no longer in service.

assignedState.description:
The current assignment state of the device.
- `ASSIGNED_TO_ACTIVATE_CONFIG`—The device was moved to the custom activate configuration.
- `ASSIGNED_TO_DEDICATED_PLATFORM`—The device is used in a dedicated platform workspace.
- `ASSIGNED_TO_SERVICE`—The device is assigned to a service.
- `UNASSIGNED`—The device is available for service provisioning.

billingAccountId.description: The billing account ID associated with the Flex device.

billingAccountName.description: The billing account name associated with the Flex device.

billingTier.description: The billing tier associated with the Flex device

category.description: The computed category of the device based on the device type, for example, Network, Storage, or Compute.

contact.description:
The contact details associated with the device.
Depending on the contact type, only the relevant fields will be populated.
- `WORKSPACE-USER`—The contact is a workspace user. In this case, the `id` and `resourceUri` fields are populated to reference the workspace user identity.
- `NON-WORKSPACE-USER`—The contact is not a workspace user. In this case, the `email`, `firstName`, `lastName`, and `phoneNumber` fields are provided for direct contact information.

createdAt.description: The date and time the device was created in UTC (RFC 3339 format).

deviceType.description: The category (type) the device belongs to.

id.description: The unique identifier of the device.

isFlex.description: A boolean that indicates whether the device has a Flex subscription or not.

macAddress.description: The media access control (MAC) address of the device

manufacturedDate.description: The date the device was manufactured in UTC (RFC 3339 format).

ownership.description: The ownership of the device based on how the device was added to the inventory.

partNumber.description: Identifier of the a device component or part.

region.description: The region of the application the device is provisioned in

serialNumber.description: The serial number of the device.

source.description: The source of device based on how it was added to the inventory.

subscriptionCategory.description: The platform subscription category assigned to the device. This field is populated only for Compute devices.

additionalProperties.description: A tag is a form of metadata applied to any resource and primarily used to categorize resources.

tenantWorkspaceId.description: The unique identifier of the tenant workspace belonging to an MSP. This field is populated only for MSP-owned inventory tenants (the MSP owns the devices and subscriptions and also manages the workspace on behalf of their customers).

updatedAt.description: The date and time the device was last updated in UTC (RFC 3339 format).

total.description: Total number of items in the collection that match the filter query, if one was provided in the request

location.description: The location ID of the device.

macAddress.description: The media access control (MAC) address of the device. This is required for claiming `NETWORK` devices.

partNumber.description: The part number of the device. This is required for claiming `COMPUTE` or `STORAGE` devices.

serialNumber.description: The serial number of the device.

key.description: A tag key is the name used to refer to the tag.

value.description: A tag value is a value applied to the tag key.

source.description: The part of the request with an issue.

count.description: Count of devices in the group

field.description: Group by attribute, dynamic value Example make, source, manufacturedDate etc

A tag value of null indicates a tag is to be deleted.

tags.description: Provide a map of `tags` to create or delete for the given `DeviceID` (or multiple `DeviceID`'s). Tags are saved with the character casing preserved (uppercase, lowercase, mixed, and so on). For example, adding a new tag with the key `Location` will fail if the Device already has a tag with the key `LOCATION` as they are considered the same key. Tag keys and tag values can comprise letters, numbers, spaces (represented in UTF-8), and only the characters: `_`, `.`, `:`, `=`, `+`, `-`, and  `@`. **NOTE:** Do not store sensitive data, such as personally identifiable information, in tags.

info.description: With the HPE GreenLake APIs for Device Management you can view, manage, and onboard devices in your workspace. The API allows you to invoke any operation or task that is available through the HPE GreenLake edge-to-cloud platform user interface.
info.title: HPE GreenLake APIs for Device Management

get.description:
The add and update device APIs are asynchronous. Use this API to find the status of the asynchronous add and update operations.

An asynchronous resource tracks the status of an asynchronous operation. An asynchronous resource that has reached a terminal state (`SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `TIMEOUT`) will no longer be accessible 24 hours after reaching the terminal state.

The `TIMEOUT` state indicates that the operation was in an `INITIALIZED` or `RUNNING` state for a period greater than the timeout set. If an asynchronous operation resource coming from an asynchronous request consists of multiple devices, a breakdown of succeeded devices and failed devices, if there are any, will be returned as the response. In this case, the device serial number identifies each device.

NOTE: You need view permissions for the Devices and Subscription service to invoke this API.

Rate limits are enforced on this API. 90 requests per minute is supported per workspace. The API returns `429` if this threshold is breached.

parameters.description: The unique identifier of an asynchronous API operation.
parameters.name: id

issues.description: Async ID is not provided.
issues.subject: id

issues.description: Authentication error - the token was expired.
issues.subject: Unauthorized

metadata.access_token: Permission is denied to perform requested operation.

issues.description: Device ID not found
issues.subject: Internal Server Error

metadata.apiRateLimit: Too Many Requests. Rate limit has exceeded a predefined value.

get.summary: Get progress or status of async operations in devices

get.description:
With this API, you can:
  • Retrieve a list of all devices in a workspace.
  • Filter devices based on conditional expressions.

NOTE: You need view permissions for Devices and Subscription service to invoke this API.

Rate limits are enforced on this API. 160 requests per minute is supported per workspace. The API returns `429` if this threshold is breached.

parameters.description:
Filter expressions consisting of simple comparison operations joined by logical operators.
| CLASS | EXAMPLES | |---------------------|----------------------------------------------------| | Types | integer, decimal, timestamp, string, boolean, null | | Comparison | eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, in | | Logical Expressions | and, or, not | The following examples are not an exhaustive list of all possible filtering options.

alternatives.description:
Return devices where a property is one of multiple values.
Example syntax, \<property> in \<value>,\<value>.
alternatives.summary: Filter for a value to have one of multiple possibilities

billingTier.description:
Return devices of a specific billing tier.
Example syntax, billingTier eq \<value>.

category.description:
Return devices of a specific category.
Example syntax, category eq \<value>.

conjunction.description:
Return devices that exactly satisfy multiple filter queries.
Example syntax, \<property> eq \<value> and \<property> eq \<value>.

currentSupportLevelEndDate.description:
Return devices whose current support level end date is on or before specific date.
Example syntax, currentSupportLevelEndDate le \<value>.
currentSupportLevelEndDate.summary: Filter example on device current support level end date

disjunction.description:
Return devices that exactly satisfy one of multiple filter queries.
Example syntax, \<property> eq \<value> or \<property> eq \<value>.
disjunction.summary: Filter on multiple non strict conditionals

equals.description:
Return devices where a property equals a value.
Example syntax, \<property> eq \<value>.

ge.description:
Return devices where a property is greater or equal to a value.
Example syntax, \<property> ge \<value>.
ge.summary: Filter with greater or equal check

isFlex.description:
Return devices that are on a flex subscription or not.
Example syntax, isFlex eq true|false.
isFlex.summary: Filter on whether device is on a flex subscription

le.description:
Return devices where a property is lesser or equal to a value.
Example syntax, \<property> ge \<value>.
le.summary: Filter with lesser or equal check

make.description:
Return devices of a specific make.
Example syntax, make eq \<value>.

manufacturedDate.description:
Return devices manufactured on or after specific date.
Example syntax, manufacturedDate ge \<value>.
manufacturedDate.summary: Filter example on device manufactured date

not.description:
Return devices where a property does not equal a value.
Example syntax, not \<property> eq \<value>.

nullFilter.description:
Return devices where a property is null or not null.
Example syntax, \<property> eq null or \<property> ne null.

source.description:
Return devices of a specific source.
Example syntax, source eq \<value>.

parameters.name: filter

parameters.description:
Filter expressions consisting of simple comparison operations joined by logical operators to be applied on the assigned tags or their values.
| CLASS | EXAMPLES | |---------------------|-----------------| | Types | string | | Comparison | eq, ne, in | | Logical Expressions | and, or, not |

alternatives.description:
Return devices containing the tag key and at least one of the specified values.
Example syntax, \<property> in \<value>,\<value>.
alternatives.summary: Filter for a value to have one of multiple possibilities

conjunction.description:
Return devices that exactly satisfy multiple filter queries applied to tag keys.
Example syntax, \<property> eq \<value> and \<property> eq \<value>.

disjunction.description:
Return devices that satisfy any of multiple filter queries applied to tag keys.
Example syntax, \<property> eq \<value> or \<property> eq \<value>.

equals.description:
Return devices where a tag key is equal to a tag value.
Example syntax, \<tagKey> eq \<tagValue>.

not.description:
Return devices where a tag key does not equal a tag value.
Example syntax, not \<property> eq \<value>.

parameters.name: filter-tags

parameters.description: A comma separated list of sort expressions. A sort expression is a property name optionally followed by a direction indicator `asc` or `desc`. The default is ascending order.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: A comma separated list of select properties to display in the response. The default is that all properties are returned.
parameters.name: select

parameters.description: Specifies the number of results to be returned. The default value is 2000.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Specifies the zero-based resource offset to start the response from. The default value is 0.
parameters.name: offset

devices_workspace_user_response.summary: Devices response with workspace user details

issues.description: Must be a numeric value which is 0 or greater.
issues.subject: limit

get.summary: Get all devices in a workspace

patch.description:
Update devices by passing one or more device IDs. The API currently supports:
  • Assigning and unassigning devices to and from a service.
  • Applying and removing subscriptions to and from devices.
To remove an application, set the `id` under `application` to `null` and 'region' to `null`. Set an empty array to the attribute `subscription` to remove a subscription.

Only one operation is supported in a single API call. For example, you cannot assign devices to an application and assign subscriptions to devices in a single API invocation. You can achieve this with two API calls.

This API provides an asynchronous response and returns `202 Accepted` if basic input validations are successful. The location header in the response provides the URI to be invoked for fetching the progress of the device update task. For details about the status fetch URL, refer to the API [Get progress or status of async operations in devices](#operation/getDevicesAsyncOperationResourceV1).

NOTE: You need edit permissions for the Devices and Subscription service to invoke this API.

Rate limits are enforced on this API. 40 requests per minute is supported per workspace. The API returns `429` if this threshold is breached.

parameters.description: Array of device resource IDs. Maximum five devices per request.
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The `dry-run` query parameter is used to perform the resource update operation (`POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`) and return a response as if the operation had completed, but without actually creating, updating, or deleting the resource. This allows you to test if the request would succeed before making the change. If set to `true`, the request is validated but not executed.
parameters.name: dry-run

issues.description: device ID is not a valid UUID.
issues.subject: id

metadata.access_token: Token provided is invalid
Permission is denied to perform requested operation.

metadata.apiRateLimit: Too Many Requests. Rate limit has exceeded a predefined value.

post.description:
Add one or more devices to a workspace. This API provides an asynchronous response and will always return `202 Accepted` if basic input validations are successful. The location header in the response provides the URI to be invoked for fetching the progress of the device addition task. For details about the status fetch URL, refer to the API [Get progress or status of async operations in devices](#operation/getDevicesAsyncOperationResourceV1).

NOTE: You need edit permissions for the Devices and Subscription service to invoke this API.

Rate limits are enforced on this API. 50 requests per minute is supported per workspace. The API returns `429` if this threshold is breached.

parameters.description: The `dry-run` query parameter is used to perform the resource update operation (`POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`) and return a response as if the operation had completed, but without actually creating, updating, or deleting the resource. This allows you to test if the request would succeed before making the change. If set to `true`, the request is validated but not executed.
parameters.name: dry-run

requestBody.description: Accepts a maximum of five devices per request, combining the count of network, compute, and storage devices.

issues.description: serial number is blank.
issues.subject: serialNumber

metadata.access_token: Token provided is invalid
Permission is denied to perform requested operation.

Add one or more devices to a workspace. This API provides an asynchronous response and will always return `202 Accepted` if basic input validations are successful. The location header in the response provides the URI to be invoked for fetching the progress of the device addition task. For details about the status fetch URL, refer to the API [Get progress or status of async operations in devices](#operation/getDevicesAsyncOperationResource).

NOTE: You need edit permissions for the Devices and Subscription service to invoke this API.

Rate limits are enforced on this API. 25 requests per minute is supported per workspace. API will result in `429` if this threshold is breached.

requestBody.description: Accepts a maximum of five devices per request, combining the count of network, compute, and storage devices.

get.description:
With this API, you can:
  • Retrieve a list of all devices in a workspace.
  • Filter devices based on conditional expressions.

NOTE: You need view permissions for Devices and Subscription service to invoke this API.

Rate limits are enforced on this API. 300 requests per minute is supported per workspace. The API returns `429` if this threshold is breached.

parameters.description:
Filter expressions consisting of simple comparison operations joined by logical operators.
| CLASS | EXAMPLES | |---------------------|----------------------------------------------------| | Types | integer, decimal, timestamp, string, boolean, null | | Comparison | eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, in | | Logical Expressions | and, or, not | The following examples are not an exhaustive list of all possible filtering options.

alternatives.description:
Return devices where a property is one of multiple values.
Example syntax, \<property> in \<value>,\<value>.
alternatives.summary: Filter for a value to have one of multiple possibilities

billingTier.description:
Return devices of a specific billing tier.
Example syntax, billingTier eq \<value>.

category.description:
Return devices of a specific category.
Example syntax, category eq \<value>.

conjunction.description:
Return devices that exactly satisfy multiple filter queries.
Example syntax, \<property> eq \<value> and \<property> eq \<value>.

currentSupportLevelEndDate.description:
Return devices whose current support level end date is on or before specific date.
Example syntax, currentSupportLevelEndDate le \<value>.
currentSupportLevelEndDate.summary: Filter example on device current support level end date

disjunction.description:
Return devices that exactly satisfy one of multiple filter queries.
Example syntax, \<property> eq \<value> or \<property> eq \<value>.
disjunction.summary: Filter on multiple non strict conditionals

equals.description:
Return devices where a property equals a value.
Example syntax, \<property> eq \<value>.

ge.description:
Return devices where a property is greater or equal to a value.
Example syntax, \<property> ge \<value>.
ge.summary: Filter with greater or equal check

isFlex.description:
Return devices that are on a flex subscription or not.
Example syntax, isFlex eq true|false.
isFlex.summary: Filter on whether device is on a flex subscription

le.description:
Return devices where a property is lesser or equal to a value.
Example syntax, \<property> le \<value>.
le.summary: Filter with lesser or equal check

make.description:
Return devices of a specific make.
Example syntax, make eq \<value>.

manufacturedDate.description:
Return devices manufactured on or after specific date.
Example syntax, manufacturedDate ge \<value>.
manufacturedDate.summary: Filter example on device manufactured date

not.description:
Return devices where a property does not equal a value.
Example syntax, not \<property> eq \<value>.

nullFilter.description:
Return devices where a property is null or not null.
Example syntax, \<property> eq null or \<property> ne null.

source.description:
Return devices of a specific source.
Example syntax, source eq \<value>.

parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: A comma separated list of sort expressions. A sort expression is a property name optionally followed by a direction indicator `asc` or `desc`. The default is ascending order.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: A comma separated list of select properties to display in the response. The default is that all properties are returned.
parameters.name: select

parameters.description: Specifies the number of results to be returned. The default value is 100 and the maximum is 500.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Specifies the zero-based resource offset to start the response from. The default value is 0.
parameters.name: offset

issues.description: Must be a numeric value which is 1 or greater.
issues.subject: limit

get.summary: Get all devices in a workspace

patch.description:
Update devices by passing one or more device IDs.

The API supports Device operations such as:
API supports updating location and tags for all types of devices, discovered and managed
Supports updating missing warranty information like warranty/currentSupportLevel/endDate, warranty/currentSupportLevel/serviceLevel for discovered devices.

The API supports earlier Device operations such as:
  • Assigning and unassigning devices to and from a service.
  • Adding, updating and removing tags to devices.
  • Archiving and un-archiving the devices.
The API endpoint supports subscription operations of applying and removing subscriptions to and from devices.

To remove an application, set the id under application to null and region to null. Set an empty array to the attribute subscription to remove a subscription. For each tag provided in the request body:
  • Tags are created and inserted into the specified devices if the provided tags do not map to null and are not already present.
  • Tags are updated with the provided if the provided tag key is already present in a device, but the provided value differs from the existing value.
  • Tags are removed from devices when a tag key is mapped to a null tag value. The tags are removed from any devices with a matching tag with the same key.
The endpoint only supports either a device or a subscription operation per API call. For example, you cannot assign devices to an application and assign subscriptions to devices in a single API call. You can achieve this with two separate API calls. However, a single API call can perform device operations together; for example, assigning an application and adding tags can be performed.

The archive device operation is incompatible with any other device operation in the same API call. For example, you cannot remove an application and archive a device using the same API call.

This is an asynchronous API endpoint and it returns the 202 Accepted response code and a Location header containing the URI of the asynchronous operation that can be used to track progress. For details about the status fetch URL, refer to the API Get progress or status of async operations in devices.

NOTE: You need edit permissions for the Devices and Subscription service to initiate this API call. Rate limits are enforced, and 40 requests per minute are supported per workspace. The API returns 429 if this threshold is breached.

parameters.description: Array of device resource IDs. Maximum twenty five devices per request.
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The `dry-run` query parameter is used to perform the resource update operation (`POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`) and return a response as if the operation had completed, but without actually creating, updating, or deleting the resource. This allows you to test if the request would succeed before making the change. If set to `true`, the request is validated but not executed.
parameters.name: dry-run

get.description: Returns devices grouped by the attribute specified in `group-by` (for example: `make`, `model`, or `source`). For date-based grouping fields, you can optionally apply `lower-threshold` and `upper-threshold` to define a date range. The response is paginated and includes each group value with its corresponding device count. Rate limits are enforced on this API. 300 requests per minute is supported per workspace. The API returns `429` if this threshold is breached.

parameters.description:
The grouping operation supports the following fields:
`make`, `source`, `category`, `manufacturedDate`, `currentSupportLevelEndDate`, `billingAccountId`, `billingTier`, `deviceType`, `ownership`, `isFlex` and `model`.
`currentSupportLevelEndDate` groups on `warranty.currentSupportLevel.endDate` and uses that nested date value.
parameters.name: group-by

parameters.description:
The filter expressions that are used to narrow down the devices before grouping.
Combine multiple conditions using `and` or `or`.
The following examples are not an exhaustive list of all possible filtering options.

Combined filter.description: Filter managed devices in a specific location
Combined filter.value: source eq 'MANAGED' and locationName eq 'San Jose'

Filter by locationId.description: Filter devices by their location resource ID

Filter by locationName.description: Filter devices assigned to a specific location

Filter by locationName (multiple).description: Filter devices in either of two locations
Filter by locationName (multiple).value: locationName eq 'San Jose' or locationName eq 'Santa Clara'

Filter by source (multiple).description: Filter devices that are either MANAGED or DISCOVERED

Filter by source (single).description: Filter all managed devices in the workspace

parameters.name: filter

parameters.description:
Lower bound for date-based grouping.

Applies only when `group-by` is a date field (`manufacturedDate` or `currentSupportLevelEndDate`).
Includes devices where the grouped date is greater than or equal to this value.
Use with `upper-threshold` to group results into four buckets: `less_than_lower`, `lower_to_upper`, `greater_than_upper`, `missing`.
parameters.name: lower-threshold

parameters.description:
Upper bound for date-based grouping.

Applies only when `group-by` is a date field (`manufacturedDate` or `currentSupportLevelEndDate`).
Includes devices where the grouped date is less than or equal to this value.
Use with `lower-threshold` to group results into four buckets: `less_than_lower`, `lower_to_upper`, `greater_than_upper`, `missing`.
parameters.name: upper-threshold

parameters.description:
Returns distinct values for the specified field, without counts.
Use this as a distinct-only variant of `group-by` (for example, get unique `make` values).
Value for select field should be the same as group-by field. For example, if you are grouping by `make`, then `select` should also be `make` to get distinct makes in the response.
Supports the same fields as `group-by`.
parameters.name: select

parameters.description: Zero-based resource offset to start the response from.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: Number of entities to return. Maximum of 100.
parameters.name: limit

issues.description: Parameter group-by is missing.
issues.subject: group-by
